programmes.show_description: Music to Die For: Lewis and Hathaway investigate a link between the murder of a professor of German history and the recent winner of a local underground boxing bout.
The Football Match: Sitcom about a bus driver. The depot's football team look forward to a five-pound bonus they have been promised if they win a match against the Basildon Bashers.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
Love Is A Many-Splendoured Thing: Classic British sitcom. Simon helps his old pal Oliver when he falls foul of his prospective mother-in-law.
The Poster: Sitcom about a bus driver and his conductor pal. The company mounts an advertising campaign to attract more passengers.
Katie learns of Gray's affair with Paul. Donna voices her concerns as Viv's behaviour becomes more unstable, and Nicola confronts Rodney about the stories he's telling Gabby.
Terry reads Rodney's book and gives Nicola the idea of sending it to a publisher. Viv learns Freddie has been sighted and begs Donna for a loan so she can rehire a PI.
Liam makes a shocking confession to Maria as the grieving couple deal with their loss. Meanwhile, Gail receives a visit from Tina and is left reeling by a revelation from Ted.
Fearing her marriage is over, Maria decides to move out, while Carla takes in Liam. Gail confides in Tina about her father's news and Leanne lands a job at the Rovers.
The Great and the Good: A rape case appears to be solved after a murder, but nothing is what it seems when Lewis and Hathaway find the upper echelons of Oxford society closing ranks.
Father's Day: Drama series set in the 1960s about a policeman in Yorkshire. A late-night encounter in a graveyard leaves Nick with a headache.
Endangered Species: Drama series set in the 1960s. A hit-and-run incident leads Nick to uncover a darker side of country life, and Greengrass goes looking for business partners.
Vixen's Run: Sir Freddy Butler's death looks like a heart attack, but Barnaby and Jones are brought to the privileged surroundings of Haddington Hall when his will disappears.
Viv faces a tense standoff with the police as Freddie grabs the twins' pram, she attempts to get help from Ross, but he finds the planted cash.
Liam faces up to his night with Carla, while Maria tells him she wants to register the baby's death and Gail has a run-in with Tina's mum when she turns up at the Platts'.
Rosie catches Liam sharing a kiss with Carla and films them on her mobile phone. Maria has a change of heart about her separation and Julie drives Fiz to breaking point.
An American in Aidensfield: Police drama series set in 1960s Yorkshire. A stranger arrives in the village and goes into partnership with Greengrass.
Bringing It All Back Home: Drama series set in 1960s Yorkshire. Changes are in the air for Nick with the arrival of a new inspector, and Kate receives a tempting job offer.
Midwife Crisis: Comedy-drama series. Martin fails to hit it off with Portwenn's new midwife when she tends to Louisa, and Bert is smitten with a new cook at his restaurant.
Do Not Disturb: Comedy-drama series. Joan starts a small bed and breakfast business to solve her farm's money problems, but she soon finds that her first guest is far from perfect.
Detective drama series. When a young woman dies in a busy park in broad daylight, the uncovering of her identity leads DS Dodds to confront secrets from his own past.
The Great and the Good: A rape case appears to be solved after a murder, but nothing is what it seems when Lewis and Hathaway find the upper echelons of Oxford society closing ranks.
The Poster: Sitcom about a bus driver and his conductor pal. The company mounts an advertising campaign to attract more passengers.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
To Sup with the Devil: Detective drama series. Lane puts herself in jeopardy when she mishandles an informant, but can Wycliffe and Kersey find her in time?
Old Crimes, New Times: Detective drama series. Wycliffe's problems multiply when an old case resurfaces and DCC Stevens seems out to prove that he is no longer up to the job.
Expiation: Feature-length drama. Lewis and Hathaway unearth a far darker case than an initial suicide verdict suggests when an Oxford housewife is found hanged in her home.
The Soul of Genius: Lewis and Hathaway are sent upon a seemingly impossible quest when a botanist accidentally digs up the body of a recently-buried professor
World War II drama starring John Thaw. A curmudgeonly widower living in a small village takes in a painfully shy evacuee from London and teaches him to read, write and draw.
King's Crystal: A glassware factory faces ruin, an accountant is murdered with a Masonic dagger, and a production of Hamlet seems to contain a message for DCI Barnaby.
The Axeman Cometh: When a rock band reform to play at the Midsomer Rocks Festival, their paranoid lead singer is certain that someone is trying to scare them off.
Death and Dust: When a doctor is killed in a hit-and-run, Barnaby realises that the real target was his senior partner and he and Jones travel to Snowdonia to unravel the truth.
Picture of Innocence: DCI Barnaby becomes the prime suspect when a local photographer is murdered - will he be able to clear his name as the evidence mounts against him?
The very first episode of the detective drama series. Newly-arrived DCI McDonald and DS Dodds try to identity a man shot dead in a mansion.
